text:
Vrijen Attawar: Howdy.
Vrijen Attawar: Howdy.
Ilse Funkhouser: Hello.
Ilse Funkhouser: Just have you heard from Logan today?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I just heard from her.
Vrijen Attawar: She is driving back so she's in Zakah.
Vrijen Attawar: I do however have something to show you.
Ilse Funkhouser: Oh, I am walking the.
Ilse Funkhouser: So showing isn't going to be the easiest thing.
Vrijen Attawar: No sweat, I can send it to you later.
Vrijen Attawar: How's the rest of your morning been?
Ilse Funkhouser: What are you going to show me?
Vrijen Attawar: Positioning documents.
Vrijen Attawar: So I have largely finalized the one pagers.
Vrijen Attawar: They are not one page.
Vrijen Attawar: These are just the positioning documents.
Vrijen Attawar: These are the overall argument that we're making and the sort of like proof points that we'll be using.
Vrijen Attawar: So love for you to just comment up on it like areas for improvement.
Ilse Funkhouser: Would you?
Ilse Funkhouser: Yes, Would you love for me to do that?
Vrijen Attawar: I would, I would know now that we're communicating like fucking pros, like like oil and water.
Vrijen Attawar: Famously good communicators.
Vrijen Attawar: No, so the, the positioning docs, I think they're of pretty decent quality.
Vrijen Attawar: They are largely synthesizing.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, it actually did a pretty decent job as well of like objections and rebuttals which I was quite happy about.
Vrijen Attawar: This is a feature, not a company fair concern.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: So it would be good for you to check that stuff out and then if everyone's looking happy with that, then yeah, I will start getting those one pagers out on mass today.
Vrijen Attawar: That's pretty much it.
Vrijen Attawar: I don't have anything else to do for standup today besides like getting hands about like 30, maybe 30ish people.
Ilse Funkhouser: Cool.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Anything from your end?
Vrijen Attawar: If not, I'll let you just enjoy your walk.
Vrijen Attawar: You had enough of me today?
Ilse Funkhouser: No, I'm like three minutes from my office.
Vrijen Attawar: Oh, nice.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay, I got the positioning in a sec.
Ilse Funkhouser: Yeah, yeah, it's not a big deal.
Ilse Funkhouser: Have you tried the scanner?
Ilse Funkhouser: And if the answer is no, then you can't answer my next question of is it sufficient for your needs.
Vrijen Attawar: I was, I was trying it and then I got a little scared so I wanted to do it with you around.
Ilse Funkhouser: Okay, that's probably a good thing to discuss and stand up.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, yeah, I was like I want to run through it but like I just didn't want to run us like a couple hundred dollar charge at this point, so.
Ilse Funkhouser: Why not?
Vrijen Attawar: Well, I just subbed in my credit card, that's why.
Vrijen Attawar: Ah, not really, but that, that technically counts.
Vrijen Attawar: No, I just, I wanted to, I wanted to implement it correctly and I also thought it would be a good chance to like just actually get more context for how the system works.
Ilse Funkhouser: Yeah.
Ilse Funkhouser: And relatedly, I'm building out I'm adding some stuff to the user's settings page that actually lets them control whether or not they want to be included in job search stuff because before now it's just been the toggle during sign up and you could never change it again.
Ilse Funkhouser: So I'm updating that there will be like a one day lag between that being updated and whether or not the user is included or excluded from role or from scan.
Ilse Funkhouser: But like it.
Ilse Funkhouser: That's good enough for now.
Vrijen Attawar: Good enough for now.
Ilse Funkhouser: Then I'm adding in additional limitations for employers that limits the number of scan requests they can have at once.
Ilse Funkhouser: As you will see or have seen, I already have a credit based system but he gave him 100 credits.
Ilse Funkhouser: There's currently nothing to stop them from doing all hundred of them then and there.
Ilse Funkhouser: The consequence of that is.
Vrijen Attawar: One run.
Ilse Funkhouser: Yeah, For now.
Ilse Funkhouser: Like if it was the most basic, I could implement it to get something out the door that.
Ilse Funkhouser: Because what you and Logan described to me was you would be charging them 50 to $100 per scale.
Ilse Funkhouser: So I just ran with that.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Which I still think we can get away with with Corridor X dependent because they're you know, making a fair bit on each role.
Vrijen Attawar: So once they got their folks on board they're pretty incentivized to scan against them dropping their time to time to.
Vrijen Attawar: Actually I think that'll be theoretically should be a really good case study because they want expedience over all else so they'll be pretty much like pushing folks to use it.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, we can, we can sort of explain the limitations of the system.
Vrijen Attawar: We can give Shivam that context.
Vrijen Attawar: He's a pretty flexible dude.
Vrijen Attawar: So.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I think that should be.
Vrijen Attawar: That should be pretty good.
Vrijen Attawar: Let me just reread these.
Vrijen Attawar: Should be good.
Ilse Funkhouser: Let me delete this, get into my office.
Ilse Funkhouser: My ski isn't working.
Vrijen Attawar: No sweat.
Vrijen Attawar: Give me one second.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm just going to hit the restroom.
Vrijen Attawar: Be right back.
Ilse Funkhouser: I'll talk to you later.
Ilse Funkhouser: I have to figure this out.
Vrijen Attawar: Nope, no sweat.
Ilse Funkhouser: Bye.
